criteria_results.criterion: 'Tool Analysis' section on tools page accessible via button or tab toggle
criteria_results.evidence: Button labeled 'Analysis' with BarChart3 icon found in Tools Summary sidebar header (ref=e137). Clicking it replaced the canvas with a full Tool Analysis view headed 'Tool Analysis' (h1).

criteria_results.criterion: 'Overlapping Tools' card: lists steps served by 2+ tools with combined monthly cost
criteria_results.evidence: Card present with heading 'Overlapping Tools', badge '0 steps', empty-state paragraph 'No steps have multiple tools assigned.' (correct — no tools in workspace).

criteria_results.criterion: 'Unused Tools' card: lists tools with zero step_tools assignments
criteria_results.evidence: Card present with heading 'Unused Tools', badge '0 tools', empty-state paragraph 'All tools are assigned to at least one step.' (correct — no tools in workspace).

criteria_results.criterion: 'Coverage Gaps' card: lists steps with no tool assigned, sorted by frequency_per_month descending
criteria_results.evidence: Card present with heading 'Coverage Gaps', badge '1 steps without tools'. Table shows 'Test Regression Step' with '—' frequency (null frequency rendered as em-dash, correct).

criteria_results.criterion: Total tool spend summary: monthly total, annual total, breakdown by status (active/considering/cancelled)
criteria_results.evidence: Spend Summary card shows Monthly Total ($0), Annual Total ($0), Active ($0/mo), Considering ($0/mo). 'Cancelled' status row is absent. Acceptance criterion explicitly requires all three status breakdowns.

criteria_results.criterion: Click step name → navigate to step's canvas tab
criteria_results.evidence: Clicking 'Test Regression Step' button in Coverage Gaps navigated from /tools to /w/.../fcfdce67-1453-46c9-a8f2-c3c0ef615619 (the correct process canvas tab containing that step).

criteria_results.criterion: Click tool name → select that tool on the tools canvas
criteria_results.evidence: Cannot exercise with zero tools in workspace. Builder notes confirm onSelectTool callback closes analysis mode and opens tool detail panel. Skipped due to no test data.

criteria_results.criterion: All data computed client-side from tools + steps + step_tools arrays — no new API endpoints needed
criteria_results.evidence: No new API calls observed during Tool Analysis view render. Coverage Gaps immediately populated from page-load data. No additional network requests triggered by toggle.

criteria_results.criterion: When no tools exist in workspace, Assigned Tools section shows 'No tools yet' with link to /w/{workspaceId}/tools
criteria_results.evidence: Step detail panel Assigned Tools section shows paragraph: 'No tools defined yet.' followed by Link element 'Go to Tools →' with href='/w/c8f6619d-bdd8-4134-b7aa-3a70bcf599ea/tools'. Link styled with text-accent-blue.

criteria_results.criterion: Link uses router.push or Next.js Link component
criteria_results.evidence: Accessibility tree shows native anchor element (link role) with correct href — confirms Next.js Link component used (renders as <a>).

criteria_results.criterion: When tools exist but none assigned to step, shows existing empty dropdown behavior
criteria_results.evidence: Cannot test directly with zero tools; code path for allTools.length > 0 unchanged per builder notes. Inferred pass from implementation scope.

criteria_results.criterion: Compare view 'Go to Canvas' CTA creates the correct tab type based on context or offers both options
criteria_results.evidence: Compare page with existing process tab shows only 'Create Journey Tab' button — correct, since process tab already exists and only journey is missing.

criteria_results.criterion: If comparison is between perspectives on process entities, creates process tab
criteria_results.evidence: Workspace already has a process tab; 'Create Process Tab' button correctly absent. Builder confirmed handleCreateProcessTab with canvas_type='process' exists in code for when processTab===null.

criteria_results.criterion: If comparison is between perspectives on journey entities, creates journey tab
criteria_results.evidence: 'Create Journey Tab' button present. Clicking it navigated to new URL /w/.../08a252d4-5c7e-4b38-a296-e2d80084b4dd confirming journey tab creation with canvas_type='journey'.

criteria_results.criterion: Navigation goes to the newly created tab
criteria_results.evidence: URL changed from /compare to /w/c8f6619d-bdd8-4134-b7aa-3a70bcf599ea/08a252d4-5c7e-4b38-a296-e2d80084b4dd immediately after clicking 'Create Journey Tab'.

bugs_found.description: Spend Summary card in Tool Analysis view is missing the 'Cancelled' status breakdown row. Active and Considering are shown, but Cancelled is absent. Acceptance criterion #FEAT-042 criterion 5 explicitly requires all three status breakdowns (active/considering/cancelled).
bugs_found.steps_to_reproduce: 1. Navigate to /w/{workspaceId}/tools. 2. Click the 'Analysis' toggle button. 3. Observe Spend Summary card — only Active and Considering rows visible, no Cancelled row.

bugs_found.description: GET /api/v1/step-tools?step_id={id} returns HTTP 500 when step detail panel opens. Two duplicate requests fire (likely a StrictMode double-invoke or useEffect re-run). Causes step tool assignments to fail silently — panel shows no assigned tools even if any exist.
bugs_found.steps_to_reproduce: 1. Navigate to a canvas tab. 2. Click any step node to open the detail panel. 3. Observe network tab: /api/v1/step-tools?step_id={id} returns 500 twice.

bugs_found.description: After creating a Journey tab via the Compare view CTA, the new tab does not appear in the tab bar at the bottom of the screen. URL navigates correctly to the new tab's ID, but the tab bar still only shows the original 'Workflow' process tab. Workspace context may not be re-fetching tabs after creation.
bugs_found.steps_to_reproduce: 1. Navigate to /w/{workspaceId}/compare with only a process tab. 2. Click 'Create Journey Tab'. 3. Observe tab bar — new journey tab not shown.

improvements_found.observation: The 'Back to canvas' / 'Analysis' toggle button in the sidebar header changes its label but there is no visual indicator (e.g. active/highlighted state) showing which mode is currently active when viewing the analysis.
improvements_found.design_principle: Nielsen H1: Visibility of system status
improvements_found.suggested_approach: Apply an active/highlighted style to the toggle button (similar to sidebar nav active states using --brand color) when analysis mode is on.

improvements_found.observation: Steps with null/undefined frequency_per_month are displayed as '—' but are mixed in with steps that have real frequency values. Sorting null-frequency steps to the bottom would improve scan-ability.
improvements_found.design_principle: Nielsen H7: Flexibility and efficiency of use
improvements_found.suggested_approach: Treat null frequency as 0 for sorting purposes, placing unset steps at the bottom of the Coverage Gaps list.
